title: address test fails on multi-address headers
description: RFC 5228 §5.1 requires that if a header contains multiple addresses separated by commas, the address test evaluates each address separately and returns true if any matches. The current eval_address_test calls address_part() on the entire raw header value, treating it as a single address. For 'To: alice@example.com, bob@example.com', a Sieve test 'address :is "To" "alice@example.com"' returns false because bare_address() returns the whole string 'alice@example.com, bob@example.com'. For angle-bracket form 'To: <alice@example.com>, <bob@example.com>', rfind('<') finds only the LAST '<', so only bob@example.com is extracted. In both cases, any address except the last (bare) or last (angle-bracket form) is unreachable. Trigger: any address test against a To/CC/BCC or other multi-address header. Bad behavior: non-last addresses silently fail to match.
